segments.text: In you, O LORD, I take refuge; let me never be put to shame.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: In your righteousness deliver me and rescue me; incline your ear to me, and save me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Be to me a rock of refuge, a strong fortress to save me, for you are my rock and my fortress.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: Be to me for a rock of habitation

segments.text: Rescue me, O my God, from the hand of the wicked, from the grasp of the unjust and cruel.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For you, O Lord, are my hope, my trust, O LORD, from my youth.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Upon you I have leaned from before my birth; you are he who took me from my mother's womb. My praise is continually of you.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I have been as a portent to many, but you are my strong refuge.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: My mouth is filled with your praise, and with your glory all the day.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Do not cast me off in the time of old age; forsake me not when my strength is spent.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For my enemies speak concerning me; those who watch for my life consult together
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: saying, 'God has forsaken him; pursue and seize him, for there is none to deliver him.'
segments.speaker: David's Enemies

segments.text: O God, be not far from me; O my God, make haste to help me!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: May my accusers be put to shame and consumed; with scorn and disgrace may they be covered who seek my hurt.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: But I will hope continually and will praise you yet more and more.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: My mouth will tell of your righteous acts, of your deeds of salvation all the day, for their number is past my knowledge.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: With the mighty deeds of the Lord GOD I will come; I will remind them of your righteousness, yours alone.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: O God, from my youth you have taught me, and I still proclaim your wondrous deeds.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: So even to old age and gray hairs, O God, do not forsake me, until I proclaim your might to another generation, your power to all those to come.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: unto old age and gray hairs

segments.text: Your righteousness, O God, reaches the high heavens. You who have done great things, O God, who is like you?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: You who have made me see many troubles and calamities will revive me again; from the depths of the earth you will bring me up again.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: You will increase my greatness and comfort me again.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I will also praise you with the psaltery, O my God; I will sing praises to you with the harp, O Holy One of Israel.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: My lips will shout for joy, when I sing praises to you; my soul also, which you have redeemed.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: And my tongue will talk of your righteous help all the day long, for they have been put to shame and disappointed who sought to do me hurt.
segments.speaker: David
